title: POST-H-018-E — Quality gate, runbook y cierre
commands: python -m devpilot_core connector sandbox run --mode replay --json --write-report

python -m pytest -p no:ddtrace tests/test_post_h_018_connector_sandbox_gate.py tests/test_post_h_018_connector_policy_binding.py tests/test_post_h_018_connector_replay.py tests/test_post_h_018_connector_sandbox_runner.py tests/test_post_h_018_connector_sandbox_policy.py tests/test_quality_gate.py tests/test_project_global_state.py -q
pass_criteria: quality-gate hardening includes and passes the connector-sandbox subgate.
Connector sandbox subgate passes with network_used=false and external_api_used=false.
connector.write_future remains blocked for every connector and connector_write_used=false.
Connector sandbox runbook documents read-only/replay/dry-run limits and no-go gates.
Test Contract Registry v1/v2 recognizes post-h-018-connector-sandbox.
POST-H-018 is closed as implemented-initial without enabling connector write, network, external APIs, remote execution or plugin execution.
block_criteria: connector-sandbox subgate allows external_api_used=true or network_used=true.
connector write can pass or connector_write_used=true appears in gate evidence.
The runbook declares connector write or external integrations production-ready.
Test Contract Registry does not include post-h-018-connector-sandbox.
risks: implemented-initial; quality gate validates local sandbox evidence but does not authorize real connector writes.
Future connector write, OAuth, webhooks or external APIs require a new ADR, threat model, approval/RBAC controls, observability and explicit backlog.
Quality gate executions may generate release outputs through existing release-reproducibility subgate; generated outputs remain excluded from clean ZIP deliverables.
evidence: ConnectorSandboxQualityGate validates exposure, replay, redaction and Policy/Approval/RBAC binding invariants.
quality-gate hardening registers connector-sandbox as critical subgate.
Connector sandbox runbook and threat model document no-go gates and explicit limitations.
